title: coriaria ruscifolia subsp. microphylla
intro: Coriaria ruscifolia subsp. microphylla, also known as the small-leaf coriaria or microphyllous coriaria, is a subspecies of the Coriaria ruscifolia plant, which belongs to the Coriariaceae family. It is a shrub native to the Mediterranean region and has been used for centuries in traditional medicine due to its medicinal properties, including anti-inflammatory, antioxidant, and antimicrobial properties. The plant has been used to treat various health issues, including gastrointestinal problems, fever, and skin conditions, offering health benefits such as reducing inflammation, improving digestion, and boosting the immune system. Coriaria ruscifolia subsp. microphylla is often used to prepare herbal teas, tinctures, and infusions, which can be consumed to harness its medicinal and health benefits.
